name: clize

metadata.name: clize
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Clize

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/clize/badge/?version=stable
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: http://clize.readthedocs.io/en/stable/?badge=stable
```

```{image} https://badges.gitter.im/Join%20Chat.svg
:alt: Join the chat at https://gitter.im/epsy/clize
:target: https://gitter.im/epsy/clize?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=pr-badge&utm_content=badge
```

```{image} https://github.com/epsy/clize/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://github.com/epsy/clize/actions/workflows/ci.yml
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/epsy/clize/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/r/epsy/clize?branch=master
```

Clize is an argument parser for [Python](https://www.python.org/). You can
use Clize as an alternative to `argparse` if you want an even easier way to
create command-line interfaces.

**With Clize, you can:**

- Create command-line interfaces by creating functions and passing them to
  `clize.run`.
- Enjoy a CLI automatically created from your functions' parameters.
- Bring your users familiar `--help` messages generated from your docstrings.
- Reuse functionality across multiple commands using decorators.
- Extend Clize with new parameter behavior.

**Here's an example:**

```python
from clize import run

def hello_world(name=None, *, no_capitalize=False):
    """Greets the world or the given name.

    :param name: If specified, only greet this person.
    :param no_capitalize: Don't capitalize the given name.
    """
    if name:
        if not no_capitalize:
            name = name.title()
        return 'Hello {0}!'.format(name)
    return 'Hello world!'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    run(hello_world)
```

The python code above can now be used on the command-line as follows:

```console
$ pip install clize
$ python3 hello.py --help
    Usage: hello.py [OPTIONS] name

    Greets the world or the given name.

    Positional arguments:
      name   If specified, only greet this person.

    Options:
      --no-capitalize   Don't capitalize the given name.

    Other actions:
      -h, --help   Show the help
$ python3 hello.py
Hello world!
$ python3 hello.py john
Hello John!
$ python3 hello.py dave --no-capitalize
Hello dave!
```

You can find the documentation and tutorials at <http://clize.readthedocs.io/>

classes.name: clize.errors.UserError
documentation: An error to be printed to the user.

classes.name: clize.errors.ArgumentError
documentation:
An error related to the arguments passed through the command-line
interface

classes.name: clize.errors.MissingRequiredArguments
documentation: Raised when required parameters have not been provided an argument

classes.name: clize.errors.TooManyArguments
documentation:
Raised when too many positional arguments have been passed for the
parameters to consume.

classes.name: clize.errors.DuplicateNamedArgument
documentation: Raised when a named option or flag has been passed twice.

classes.name: clize.errors.UnknownOption
documentation: Raised when a named argument has no matching parameter.

classes.name: clize.errors.MissingValue
documentation: Raised when an option received no value.

classes.name: clize.errors.NotEnoughValues
documentation:
Raised when MultiOptionParameter is given less values than its min
parameter.

classes.name: clize.errors.TooManyValues
documentation:
Raised when MultiOptionParameter is given more values than its max
parameter.

classes.name: clize.errors.CliValueError
documentation:
Specialization of `ValueError` for showing a message to the user along
with the error rather than just the incorrect value.

classes.name: clize.errors.BadArgumentFormat
documentation: Raised when an argument cannot be converted to the correct format.

classes.name: clize.errors.ArgsBeforeAlternateCommand
documentation:
Raised when there are arguments before a non-fallback alternate
command.

functions.name: clize.parameters.mapped
documentation:
Creates an annotation for parameters that maps input values to Python
objects.

```
param sequence values

A sequence of pyobj, names, description tuples.
For each item, the user can specify a name from names and the
parameter will receive the corresponding pyobj value.
description is used when listing the possible values.

param str list_name

The value the user can use to show a list of possible
values and their description.

param bool case_sensitive

Force case-sensitiveness for the input values.
The default is to guess based on the contents of values.
```

```{literalinclude} /../examples/mapped.py
:lines: 4-15
```

functions.name: clize.parameters.one_of
documentation:
Creates an annotation for a parameter that only accepts the given
values.

```
param values

value, description tuples, or just the accepted values

param str list_name

The value the user can use to show a list of possible
values and their description.

param bool case_sensitive

Force case-sensitiveness for the input values.
The default is to guess based on the contents of values.
```

functions.name: clize.parameters.multi
documentation:
For option parameters, allows the parameter to be repeated on the
command-line with an optional minimum or maximum. For `*args`-like
parameters, just adds the optional bounds.

```{literalinclude} /../examples/multi.py
:lines: 4-10
```

functions.name: clize.parameters.argument_decorator
documentation:
Decorates a function to create an annotation for adding parameters
to qualify another.

```{literalinclude} /../examples/argdeco.py
:lines: 5-24
```

functions.name: clize.parameters.value_inserter
documentation:
Create an annotation that hides a parameter from the command-line
and always gives it the result of a function.

```
param function value_factory

Called to determine the value to provide
for the parameter. The current  instance
is passed as argument, ie. value_factory(ba).
```

functions.name: clize.parameters.pass_name
documentation:
Parameters decorated with this will receive the executable name as
argument.

This can be either the path to a Python file, or `python -m some.module`. It is also appended with sub-command names.

classes.name: clize.parameters.MultiOptionParameter
documentation: Named parameter that can collect multiple values.

classes.name: clize.parameters.InserterParameter
documentation:
Parameter that provides an argument to the called function without
requiring an argument on the command line.

functions.name: clize.converters.datetime
documentation:
Parses a date into a `datetime` value

Requires `dateutil` to be installed.

functions.name: clize.converters.file
documentation:
Takes a file argument and provides a Python object that opens a file

```
def main(in_: file(), out: file(mode='w')):
    with in_ as infile, out as outfile:
        outfile.write(infile.read())
```

```
param stdio

If this value is passed as argument, it will be interpreted
as stdin or stdout depending on the mode parameter supplied.

param keep_stdio_open

If true, does not close the file if it is stdin
or stdout.
```

Other arguments will be relayed to `io.open`.

You can specify a default file name using `clize.Parameter.cli_default`:

```
def main(inf: (file(), Parameter.cli_default("-"))):
    with inf as f:
        print(f)
```

```console
$ python3 ./main.py
<_io.TextIOWrapper name='<stdin>' mode='r' encoding='UTF-8'>
```

functions.name: clize.runner._dispatcher_helper
documentation: alias for clize.help.DispatcherHelper, avoiding circular import

functions.name: clize.runner._get_executable
documentation: Get the shortest invocation for a given command

functions.name: clize.runner._fix_argv
documentation: Tries to restore the given sys.argv to something closer to what the user would've typed

functions.name: clize.runner.run
documentation:
Runs a function or {ref}`CLI object<cli-object>` with `args`, prints
the return value if not None, or catches the given exception types as well
as `clize.UserError` and prints their string representation, then exit with
the appropriate status code.

```
param sequence args

The arguments to pass the CLI, for instance
('./a_script.py', 'spam', 'ham'). If unspecified, uses .

param catch

Catch these exceptions and print their string representation
rather than letting Python print an uncaught exception traceback.

type catch

sequence of exception classes

param bool exit

If true, exit with the appropriate status code once the
function is done.

param file out

The file in which to print the return value of the
command. If unspecified, uses 

param file err

The file in which to print any exception text.
If unspecified, uses .
```

classes.name: clize.runner.Clize

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.__init__
documentation:
---
param bool hide_help: |-
  Mark the parameters used to trigger the help
  as undocumented.
param help_names: Names to use to trigger the help.
param helper_class: |-
  A callable to produce a helper object to be
  used when the help is triggered. If unset, uses `.ClizeHelp`.
param sequence alt: Alternate actions the CLI will handle.
type help_names: sequence of strings
type helper_class: a type like `.ClizeHelp`
---

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.parameters
documentation:
Returns the parameters used to instantiate this class, minus the
wrapped callable.

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.keep
documentation:
Instead of wrapping the decorated callable, sets its `cli`
attribute to a `.Clize` instance. Useful if you need to use the
decorator but must still be able to call the function regularly.

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.as_is
documentation:
Returns a CLI object which uses the given callable with no
translation.

The following parameters improve the decorated object's compatibility
with Clize's help output:

```
param description

A description for the command.

param usages

A list of usages for the command.
```

:::{seealso}
{ref}`interop`
:::

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.get_cli
documentation:
Makes an attempt to discover a command-line interface for the
given object.

(cli-object)=

The process used is as follows:

1. If the object has a `cli` attribute, it is used with no further
   transformation.
2. If the object is callable, `.Clize` or whichever object this
   class method is used from is used to build a CLI. `**kwargs` are
   forwarded to its initializer.
3. If the object is iterable, `.SubcommandDispatcher` is used on
   the object, and its `cli <.SubcommandDispatcher.cli>` method
   is used.

Most notably, `clize.run` uses this class method in order to interpret
the given object(s).

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.cli
documentation: Returns the object itself, in order to be selected by `.get_cli`

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.helper
documentation:
A cli object(usually inherited from `.help.Help`) when the user
requests a help message. See the constructor for ways to affect this
attribute.

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.signature
documentation: The `.parser.CliSignature` object used to parse arguments.

methods.name: clize.runner.Clize.read_commandline
documentation:
Reads the command-line arguments from args and returns a tuple
with the callable to run, the name of the program, the positional
and named arguments to pass to the callable.

:raises: `.ArgumentError`

Wraps a function into a CLI object that accepts command-line arguments
and translates them to match the wrapped function's parameters.

modules.name: clize.help
documentation:
`clize.help` manages the generation of help messages obtained using `--help`.

`.HelpCli` is the command-line interface for it. It is injected as an extra
alternate option by `.Clize`. It can be replaced using `.Clize`'s
`helper_class` parameter.

`.HelpForClizeDocstring` constructs the help for clize's docstring format.
It is invoked by `.HelpCli.get_help` method. It can be swapped with the
`builder` parameter of `.HelpCli`.

It uses `sigtools.specifiers.signature` to obtain which functions document the
parameters, `.elements_from_clize_docstring` and `.helpstream_from_elements`
process the docstring so it can be fed to
`.HelpForClizeDocstring.add_docstring`.
clize -- A command-line argument parser for Python
Copyright (C) 2011-2022 by Yann Kaiser and contributors. See AUTHORS and
COPYING for details.

functions.name: clize.help.elements_from_clize_docstring
documentation:
Converts a string to an iterable of element tuples such as
`(EL_FREE_TEXT, "text", False)`.

The result is suitable for `helpstream_from_elements`.

See below for which tuples are produced/understood.

functions.name: clize.help.helpstream_from_elements
documentation:
Transforms an iterable of non-explicit `EL_*` elements to an iterable of
explicit `HELP_*` elements.

The result is suitable for `HelpForClizeDocstring.add_helpstream`.

functions.name: clize.help._findall_iter
documentation: Backwards compatibility pre Docutils 0.19

functions.name: clize.help._document_from_sphinx_docstring
documentation:
Reads a Sphinx.autodoc-compatible docstring into something
`helpstream_from_elements` can process.

variables.name: clize.help.LABEL_POS
documentation: The label for positional parameters

variables.name: clize.help.LABEL_OPT
documentation: The default label for named parameters

variables.name: clize.help.LABEL_ALT
documentation: The label for alternate actions like ``--help``

variables.name: clize.help.EL_LABEL
documentation:
``(EL_LABEL, "label")``

Indicates that the subsequent `EL_PARAM_DESC` elements are under a section
label.

variables.name: clize.help.EL_FREE_TEXT
documentation:
`(EL_FREE_TEXT, "paragraph", is_preformatted)`

Designates some free text. May be converted into header text, footer text, or
additional paragraphs after a parameter depending on context.

The last free text elements at the end of a docstring are always considered
to be the footer rather than additional paragraphs after a parameter.

`is_preformatted` is a boolean that indicates that the paragraph should not
be reformatted.

variables.name: clize.help.EL_PARAM_DESC
documentation:
``(EL_PARAM_DESC, "param", "paragraph")``

Designates the description for a parameter.

variables.name: clize.help.EL_AFTER
documentation:
``(EL_AFTER, "param", "paragraph", is_preformatted)``

Explicitly designates an additional paragraph after a parameter. Unlike
`EL_FREE_TEXT`, this cannot be confused for a footer paragraph.

variables.name: clize.help.HELP_HEADER
documentation:
``(HELP_HEADER, "paragraph", is_preformatted)``

Designates a paragraph that appears before the parameter descriptions.

variables.name: clize.help.HELP_FOOTER
documentation:
``(HELP_FOOTER, "paragraph", is_preformatted)``

Designates a paragraph that appears after the parameter descriptions.

variables.name: clize.help.HELP_PARAM_DESC
documentation:
``(HELP_PARAM_DESC, "param", "label", "paragraph")``

Designates a parameter description. If no label was specified `None` should take its
place.

variables.name: clize.help.HELP_PARAM_AFTER
documentation:
``(HELP_PARAM_AFTER, "param", "paragraph", is_preformatted)``

Designates a paragraph after a parameter description.

classes.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters.blank_from_signature
documentation:
Creates a blank instance with placeholders for the parameters in
`signature`.

The parameters are sorted into three sections:

```






Positional parameters



Named parameters



Alternate options (e.g. --help)
```

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters.show_usage
documentation:
Returns a summary overview of the command's parameters.

Option parameters are collapsed as `[OPTIONS]` in the output.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters.usages
documentation: Returns an iterable of all possible complete usage patterns

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters.show_full_usage
documentation:
Returns an iterable of all possible complete usage patterns
including the command name

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForParameters.show_help
documentation: Produce the full help.

Stores and displays help for a CLI with positional parameters,
named parameters and/or alternate actions designated by a named parameter

Example output in relation to attribute names:

```
header

section:
    param1   Param1 description

After param2

    param2   Param2 description

footer
```

```
.. attribute:: header
    :annotation: = []

    A list of strings representing paragraphs in the help
    header/description.
```

```
.. attribute:: footer
    :annotation: = []

    A list of strings representing paragraphs in the help
    footer after everything else.
```

```
.. attribute:: sections
    :annotation: = OrderedDict("section_name" =>
        OrderedDict("param_name" =>
            (param, "description")))

    Maps section names to parameters and their help.
```

```
.. attribute:: after
    :annotation: = {"param_name": ["paragraph"]}

    Maps parameter names to additional paragraphs.
```

classes.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.from_subject
documentation:
Constructs a `HelpForClizeDocstring` instance and populates it with
data from a `.Clize` instance.

It uses the parameters' `.parser.Parameter.prepare_help` and reads
the docstrings of functions from which the parameters originate.

```
param .Clize subject

The  instance to document.

param object owner

The object of which subject is a member of,
or .

This typically has a value if a CLI is defined as a class:

class MyCli(object):
    @clize.Clize
    def cli(self, param):
        ...

owner would refer to an instance of MyCli.
```

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.add_from_parameters
documentation: Uses `.parser.Parameter.prepare_help` on an iterable of parameters

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.add_from_parameter_sources
documentation:
Processes the docstrings of the functions that have parameters
in `subject` and adds their information to this instance.

```
param .Clize subject

the Clize runner to document
```

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.add_docstring
documentation:
Parses and integrates info from a docstring to this instance.

:param str docstring: The docstring to be read. Must be de-indented
    using something like `inspect.cleandoc`.
:param set pnames: If not `None`, only add info about these parameter
    names.
:param bool primary: Add headers and footers from this docstring.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.parse_docstring
documentation: Alias of `add_docstring` for backwards compatibility.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForAutodetectedDocstring.add_helpstream
documentation:
Add an iterable of tuples starting with `HELP_` to this instance.

```
param iterable stream

An iterable of (HELP_*, ...) tuples,
as produced by 

param set pnames

If not , only add info about these parameter
names.

param bool primary

Add headers and footers from this docstring.
```

Builds generic parameter help from the docstrings of Clize instances

Uses a custom docstring format. See {ref}`clize docstring`.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForClizeDocstring.add_docstring
documentation: Parses a Clize docstring.

classes.name: clize.help.HelpForSphinxDocstring
documentation:
Builds generic parameter help from the docstrings of Clize instances

Understands docstrings written for Sphinx's {rst:dir}`autodoc
<sphinx:autofunction>`.

classes.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands.from_subject
documentation:
Constructs a `.HelpForSubcommands` instance and populates it with
data from a `.Clize` instance.

It uses the parameters' `.parser.Parameter.prepare_help` and reads
the docstrings of functions from which the parameters originate.

:param .Clize subject: The `.Clize` instance to document.
:param .SubcommandDispatcher owner: The subcommand dispatcher being
    documented.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands.show_help
documentation: Produce the full help.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands.show_usage
documentation: Returns a summary overview of the dispatcher's command format.

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands.show_full_usage
documentation:
Returns an iterable of all possible complete usage patterns
for subcommands including the command name

methods.name: clize.help.HelpForSubcommands.usages
documentation:
Returns an iterable of all possible complete usage patterns for
all subcommands

Stores help for subcommand dispatchers.

```
.. attribute:: subcommands

    An ordered mapping of the subcommand names to their description.
```

```
.. attribute:: header

    Iterable of paragraphs for the help header.
```

```
.. attribute:: footer

    Iterable of paragraphs for the help footnotes.
```

modules.name: clize.parser
documentation: interpret function signatures and read commandline arguments
clize -- A command-line argument parser for Python
Copyright (C) 2011-2022 by Yann Kaiser and contributors. See AUTHORS and
COPYING for details.

functions.name: clize.parser.value_converter
documentation:
Callables decorated with this can be used as a value converter.

```
param str name

Use this name to designate the parameter value type.

This should be in uppercase, with as few words as possible and no word
delimiters.

The default is the name of the decorated function or type, modified to
follow this rule.

param bool convert_default

If true, the value converter will be called
with the default parameter value if none was supplied. Otherwise, the
default parameter is used as-is.

Make sure to handle  appropriately if you override this.

source:19: (INFO/1) No directive entry for "deprecated" in module "docutils.parsers.rst.languages.en".
Trying "deprecated" as canonical directive name.

source:19: (ERROR/3) Unknown directive type "deprecated".

.. deprecated:: 5.0

    Instruct users to
    use the `clize.Parameter.cli_default` annotation instead
    to supply values that are converted by a CLI value converter.


param function convert_convert_default_filter

If convert_default is true, controls when the converter is called.
The converter is used only if the given function returns true.

source:30: (INFO/1) No directive entry for "deprecated" in module "docutils.parsers.rst.languages.en".
Trying "deprecated" as canonical directive name.

source:30: (ERROR/3) Unknown directive type "deprecated".

.. deprecated:: 5.0

    Avoid ``convert_default`` completely.
```

See {ref}`value converter`.

functions.name: clize.parser.parameter_converter
documentation:
Decorates a callable to be interpreted as a parameter converter
when passed as an annotation.

It will be called with an `inspect.Parameter` object and a sequence of
objects passed as annotations, without the parameter converter itself.
It is expected to return a `clize.parser.Parameter` instance or
`Parameter.IGNORE`.

functions.name: clize.parser.use_class
documentation:
Creates a parameter converter similar to the default converter that
picks one of 4 factory functions depending on the type of parameter.

```
param name str

A name to set on the parameter converter.

param pos

The parameter factory for positional parameters.

param varargs

The parameter factory for *args-like parameters.

param named

The parameter factory for keyword parameters.

param varkwargs

The parameter factory for **kwargs-like parameters.

type pos

callable that returns a  instance

type varargs

callable that returns a  instance

type named

callable that returns a  instance

type varkwargs

callable that returns a  instance

param collections.abc.Mapping kwargs

additional arguments to pass
to the chosen factory.
```

functions.name: clize.parser.use_mixin
documentation:
Like `use_class`, but creates classes inheriting from `cls` and
one of `PositionalParameter`, `ExtraPosArgsParameter`, and
`OptionParameter`

```
param cls

The class to use as mixin.

param collections.abc.Mapping kwargs

additional arguments to pass
to the chosen factory.

param name

The name to use for the converter.  Uses cls's name
if unset.
```

variables.name: clize.parser.default_converter
documentation: The default parameter converter. It is described in detail in {ref}`default-converter`.

classes.name: clize.parser.Parameter

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.read_argument
documentation:
Reads one or more arguments from `ba.in_args` from position `i`.

```
param clize.parser.CliBoundArguments ba

The bound arguments object this call is expected to mutate.

param int i

The current position in ba.args.
```

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.apply_generic_flags
documentation:
Called after `read_argument` in order to set attributes on `ba`
independently of the arguments.

```
param clize.parser.CliBoundArguments ba

The bound arguments object this call is expected to mutate.
```

The base implementation of this method applies the `last_option`
setting if applicable and discards itself from
`CliBoundArguments.unsatisfied`

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.unsatisfied
documentation:
Called after processing arguments if this parameter required
and not discarded from `.CliBoundArguments.unsatisfied`.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.post_parse
documentation: Called after all arguments are processed successfully.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.get_all_names
documentation: Return a string with all of this parameter's names.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.get_full_name
documentation: Return a string that designates this parameter.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.__str__
documentation: Return a string to represent this parameter in cli usage.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.show_help
documentation:
Called by `~clize.help.ClizeHelp` to produce the parameter's
description in the help output.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.show_help_parens
documentation:
Return a string to complement a parameter's description in the
`--help` output.

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.help_parens
documentation:
Return an iterable of strings to complement a parameter's
description in the `--help` output. Used by `.show_help_parens`

methods.name: clize.parser.Parameter.prepare_help
documentation:
Called by `~clize.help.ClizeHelp` to allow parameters to
complement the help.

:param: clize.help.ClizeHelp helper: The object charged with
    displaying the help.

class_variables.name: clize.parser.Parameter.required
documentation: Is this parameter required?

class_variables.name: clize.parser.Parameter.is_alternate_action
documentation:
Should this parameter appear as an alternate action or as a regular
parameter?

class_variables.name: clize.parser.Parameter.extras
documentation: Iterable of extra parameters this parameter incurs

Represents a CLI parameter.

```
param str display_name

The 'default' representation of the parameter.

param bool undocumented

If true, hides the parameter from the command help.

param last_option

If , the parameter will set the 
flag on the bound arguments.
```

Also available as `clize.Parameter`.

classes.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithSourceEquivalent
documentation:
Parameter that relates to a function parameter in the source.

:param str argument_name: The name of the parameter.

classes.name: clize.parser.HelperParameter
documentation:
Parameter that doesn't appear in CLI signatures but is used for
instance as the `.sticky` attribute of the bound arguments.

classes.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.required
documentation: Tells if the parameter has no default value.

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.read_argument
documentation:
Uses `.get_value`, `.coerce_value` and `.set_value` to process
an argument

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.set_value
documentation:
Save the value for this parameter

Usually accomplished by adding to `ba.args <CliBoundArguments.args>` or
`ba.kwargs <CliBoundArguments.kwargs>`.

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.coerce_value
documentation:
Coerces `arg` using the `.conv` function. Raises
`.errors.BadArgumentFormat` if the coercion function raises
`ValueError`.

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.get_value
documentation:
Retrieves the "value" part of the argument in `ba` at
position `i`.

methods.name: clize.parser.ParameterWithValue.help_parens
documentation: Shows the default value in the parameter description.

A parameter that takes a value from the arguments, with possible
default and/or conversion.

```
param callable conv

A callable to convert the value or raise .
Defaults to .

param default

A default value for the parameter or .
```

classes.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.alias_key
documentation:
Sort key function to order aliases in source order, but with short
forms(one dash) first.

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.get_all_names
documentation: Retrieves all aliases.

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.short_name
documentation:
Retrieves the shortest alias for displaying the parameter
signature.

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.get_full_name
documentation: Uses the shortest name instead of the display name.

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.redispatch_short_arg
documentation:
Processes the rest of an argument as if it was a new one prefixed
with one dash.

For instance when `-a` is a flag in `-abcd`, the object implementing
it will call this to proceed as if `-a -bcd` was passed.

methods.name: clize.parser.NamedParameter.get_value
documentation:
Fetches the value after the `=` (`--opt=val`) or in the
next argument (`--opt val`).

Equivalent of a keyword-only parameter in Python.

```
param aliases

The arguments that trigger this parameter. The first alias
is used to refer to the parameter. The first one is picked as
 if unspecified.

type aliases

sequence of strings
```

classes.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.read_argument
documentation:
Stores the argument in `CliBoundArguments.kwargs` if it isn't
already present.

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.set_value
documentation: Set the value in `ba.kwargs <CliBoundArguments.kwargs>`

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.format_type
documentation: Returns a string designation of the value type.

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.format_argument
documentation: Format the value type for the parameter

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.get_all_names
documentation: Appends the value type to all aliases.

methods.name: clize.parser.OptionParameter.get_full_name
documentation: Appends the value type to the shortest alias.
A named parameter that takes an argument.

classes.name: clize.parser.FlagParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.FlagParameter.read_argument
documentation:
Overrides `NamedParameter`'s value-getting behavior to allow no
argument to be passed after the flag is named.

methods.name: clize.parser.FlagParameter.format_argument
documentation:
Formats the argument value type for usage messages.

This is usually empty unless the flag uses a non-boolean flag.

class_variables.name: clize.parser.FlagParameter.false_triggers
documentation:
Values for which `--flag=X` will consider the argument false and
will pass `.false_value` to the function. In all other cases `.value`
is passed.

A named parameter that takes no argument.

```
param value

The value when the argument is present.

param false_value

The value when the argument is given one of the
false value triggers using --param=xyz.
```

classes.name: clize.parser.IntOptionParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.IntOptionParameter.read_argument
documentation: Handles redispatching after a numerical value.

A named parameter that takes an integer as argument. The short form
of it can be chained with the short form of other named parameters.

classes.name: clize.parser.PositionalParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.PositionalParameter.set_value
documentation:
Stores the argument at the appropriate position
in `ba.args <CliBoundArguments.args>`.

If `ba.args <CliBoundArguments.args>` is not filled up to this
parameter's position yet, it will be filled with default values.

```
raises ValueError

when setting a parameter after unsatisfied
parameters with no default value.
```

methods.name: clize.parser.PositionalParameter.help_parens
documentation:
Puts the value type in parenthesis since it isn't shown in
the parameter's signature.
Equivalent of a positional-only parameter in Python.

classes.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.required
documentation: Returns if there is a minimum amount of values required.

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.get_collection
documentation: Return an object that new values will be appended to.

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.read_argument
documentation:
Reset read_argument to avoid hitting `OptionParameter.read_argument`
which checks for duplicate parameters

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.set_value
documentation:
Adds passed argument to the collection returned
by `get_collection`.

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.apply_generic_flags
documentation: Doesn't automatically mark the parameter as satisfied.

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.unsatisfied
documentation:
Lets `errors.MissingRequiredArguments` be raised or raises
`errors.NotEnoughValues` if arguments were passed but not enough
to meet `.min`.

methods.name: clize.parser.MultiParameter.get_full_name
documentation: Adds an ellipsis to the parameter name.
Parameter that can collect multiple values.

classes.name: clize.parser.ExtraPosArgsParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.ExtraPosArgsParameter.get_collection
documentation: Uses `CliBoundArguments.args` to collect the remaining arguments.

methods.name: clize.parser.ExtraPosArgsParameter.apply_generic_flags
documentation:
Sets itself as sticky parameter so that `errors.TooManyArguments`
is not raised when processing further parameters.

Parameter that forwards all remaining positional arguments to the
callee.

Used to convert `*args`-like parameters.

classes.name: clize.parser.AppendArguments

methods.name: clize.parser.AppendArguments.get_collection
documentation: Uses `CliBoundArguments.args` to collect the remaining arguments.

Helper parameter that collects multiple values to be passed as
positional arguments to the callee.

Similar to `ExtraPosArgsParameter` but does not correspond to a parameter
in the source.

classes.name: clize.parser.IgnoreAllArguments

methods.name: clize.parser.IgnoreAllArguments.read_argument
documentation: Does nothing, ignoring all arguments processed.

Helper parameter for `.FallbackCommandParameter` that ignores the
remaining arguments.

classes.name: clize.parser.FallbackCommandParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.FallbackCommandParameter.description
documentation:
Use `.func`'s docstring to provide the parameter
description.

methods.name: clize.parser.FallbackCommandParameter.read_argument
documentation:
Clears all processed arguments, sets up `.func` to be called later,
and lets all remaining arguments be collected as positional if this
was the first argument.

Parameter that sets an alternative function when triggered. When used
as an argument other than the first all arguments are discarded.

classes.name: clize.parser.AlternateCommandParameter

methods.name: clize.parser.AlternateCommandParameter.read_argument
documentation:
Raises an error when this parameter is used after other arguments
have been given.

Parameter that sets an alternative function when triggered. Cannot
be used as any argument but the first.

classes.name: clize.parser.CliSignature

methods.name: clize.parser.CliSignature.from_signature
documentation:
Takes a signature object and returns an instance of this class
deduced from it.

:param inspect.Signature sig: The signature object to use.
:param iterable extra: Extra parameter instances to include.

methods.name: clize.parser.CliSignature.convert_parameter
documentation: Convert a Python parameter to a CLI parameter.

methods.name: clize.parser.CliSignature.read_arguments
documentation:
Returns a `.CliBoundArguments` instance for this CLI signature
bound to the given arguments.

:param sequence args: The CLI arguments, minus the script name.
:param str name: The script name.

A collection of parameters that can be used to translate CLI arguments
to function arguments.

```
param iterable parameters

The parameters to use.
```

```
.. attribute:: converter
   :annotation: = clize.parser.default_converter

   The converter used by default in case none is present in the
   annotations.
```

```
.. attribute:: parameters

    An ordered dict of all parameters of this cli signature.
```

```
.. attribute:: positional

    List of positional parameters.
```

```
.. attribute:: alternate

    List of parameters that initiate an alternate action.
```

```
.. attribute:: named

    List of named parameters that aren't in `.alternate`.
```

```
.. attribute:: aliases
    :annotation: = {}

    Maps parameter names to `NamedParameter` instances.
```

```
.. attribute:: required
    :annotation: = set()

    A set of all required parameters.
```

classes.name: clize.parser._SeekFallbackCommand
documentation:
Context manager that tries to seek a fallback command if an error was
raised.

classes.name: clize.parser.CliBoundArguments

methods.name: clize.parser.CliBoundArguments.process_arguments
documentation:
Process the arguments in `.in_args`, setting the `.func`,
`.post_name`, `.args` and `.kwargs` attributes as a result.

This methods reads `str`'s from `.in_args`. For each one, it finds the
relevant `Parameter` instance in `.posparam` or `.namedparam` and
delegates processing to it

class_variables.name: clize.parser.CliBoundArguments.threshold
documentation:
Similarity threshold below which argument suggestions are dropped. Used
when the user enters an incorrect argument and we try to suggest a valid
argument instead.

Command line arguments bound to a `.CliSignature` instance.

```
param CliSignature sig

The signature to bind against.

param sequence args

The CLI arguments, minus the script name.

param str name

The script name.
```

```
.. attribute:: sig

    The signature being bound to.
```

```
.. attribute:: in_args

    The CLI arguments, minus the script name.
```

```
.. attribute:: name

    The script name.
```

```
.. attribute:: args
    :annotation: = []

    List of arguments to pass to the target function.
```

```
.. attribute:: kwargs
    :annotation: = {}

    Mapping of named arguments to pass to the target function.
```

```
.. attribute:: meta
    :annotation: = {}

    A dict for parameters to store data for the duration of the
    argument processing.
```

```
.. attribute:: func
    :annotation: = None

    If not `None`, replaces the target function.
```

```
.. attribute:: post_name
    :annotation: = []

    List of words to append to the script name when passed to the target
    function.
```

The following attributes only exist while arguments are being processed:

```
.. attribute:: posparam
   :annotation: = iter(sig.positional)

   The iterator over the positional parameters used to process positional
   arguments.
```

```
.. attribute:: namedparam
   :annotation: = dict(sig.aliases)

   The `dict` used to look up named parameters from their names.

   It is copied here so that the argument parsing process may add or remove
   parameters without affecting the original signature.
```

```
.. attribute:: unsatisfied
   :annotation: = set(sig.required)

   Required parameters that haven't yet been satisfied.
```

```
.. attribute:: not_provided
   :annotation: = set(sig.optional)

   Optional parameters that haven't yet received a value.
```

```
.. attribute:: sticky
   :annotation: = None

   If not `None`, a parameter that will keep receiving positional
   arguments.
```

```
.. attribute:: posarg_only
   :annotation: = False

   Arguments will always be processed as positional when this is set to
   `True`.
```

```
.. attribute:: skip
   :annotation: = 0

   Amount of arguments to skip.
```
